walkthrough.title: Place the Board
walkthrough.text: Find the main game board and place it in the center of the table where everyone can reach it. This board shows four towers on the left side, a harvest area, a production area, a market, and a council palace. You will also see three tracks running along the edges: Victory Points, Military Points, and Faith Points, plus a Turn Order track.
walkthrough.image_caption: Main game board placed centrally

walkthrough.title: Sort the Development Cards
walkthrough.text: Find the 96 Development Cards. These come in four types identified by color: green Territory cards, yellow Building cards, blue Character cards, and purple Venture cards. Each card also has a period number on its back (I, II, or III). Separate them by type AND period, giving you 12 mini-decks of 8 cards each. Shuffle each mini-deck separately. Then for each card type, stack them: Period III on the bottom, Period II in the middle, Period I on top. You should now have 4 decks of 24 cards, one per type. Place these decks next to the board near their matching towers.
walkthrough.image_caption: Four development card decks sorted by type and period

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Excommunication Tiles
walkthrough.text: Find the 21 Excommunication Tiles. Separate them by period number on the back (I, II, III). Shuffle each period's tiles separately. Draw one tile from each period and place it face up in the corresponding excommunication space on the board. There are three spaces, one for each period. Put all leftover excommunication tiles back in the box without looking at them.
walkthrough.image_caption: Three excommunication tiles placed on the board

walkthrough.title: Set Up the General Supply
walkthrough.text: Place all the wooden resource tokens and coins next to the board. These form the general supply that everyone draws from. You have wood tokens (16 small worth 1 each, 4 large worth 5 each), stone tokens (16 small worth 1, 4 large worth 5), servant tokens (18 small worth 1, 5 large worth 5), and coins in denominations of 1 and 5. Resources are considered unlimited. If you run out, improvise with substitutes.
walkthrough.image_caption: Resource supply organized beside the board

walkthrough.title: Place the Dice
walkthrough.text: Find the 3 dice: one white, one black, and one orange. Place them next to the board in their designated spaces. These dice will be rolled at the start of each round to determine the value of your family members.
walkthrough.image_caption: Three colored dice placed on the board

walkthrough.title: Adjust for Player Count
walkthrough.text: If you are playing with 2 players, find all 4 Covering Tiles and place them on the action spaces marked with both a special symbol. These block certain tower floors, market spaces, and the large harvest/production spaces. If you are playing with 3 players, place only the 2 small Covering Tiles on the spaces marked with a single symbol. With 4 players, do not use any covering tiles.
walkthrough.image_caption: Covering tiles placed for 2 or 3 player games

walkthrough.title: Choose Colors and Distribute Player Components
walkthrough.text: Each player chooses a color and receives: one Personal Board, one Personal Bonus Tile (for the basic game use the standard side; for advanced play, draft them in reverse turn order), 3 colored Family Member pawns of their color, 1 uncolored Family Member pawn with their color's sticker, 3 Excommunication Cubes of their color, and 4 Marker Discs of their color.
walkthrough.image_caption: Player components laid out by color

walkthrough.title: Place Marker Discs on Tracks
walkthrough.text: Each player places one marker disc on the 0 space of the Victory Points track, one on the 0 space of the Military Points track, one on the 0 space of the Faith Points track, and the last disc on the Turn Order track. Keep your Personal Board and Personal Bonus Tile in front of you with your family members and excommunication cubes nearby.
walkthrough.image_caption: Marker discs on starting positions of all tracks

walkthrough.title: Determine Turn Order
walkthrough.text: Randomly determine the starting turn order. Place each player's marker disc on the Turn Order track in the chosen order. The first player will roll the dice each round and take the first action.
walkthrough.image_caption: Turn order track with player markers

walkthrough.title: Distribute Starting Resources
walkthrough.text: Every player receives 2 wood, 2 stone, and 3 servants from the general supply. Place these on your Personal Board. Then distribute coins based on turn order: 1st player gets 5 coins, 2nd player gets 6 coins, 3rd player gets 7 coins, and 4th player gets 8 coins. Later players get more coins to offset the disadvantage of going later. You are now ready to begin Round 1.
walkthrough.image_caption: Starting resources distributed to each player

walkthrough.title: Advanced Setup: Leader Cards
walkthrough.text: If playing with the advanced rules, shuffle the 20 Leader Cards and deal 4 to each player. Each player drafts by choosing 1 card to keep and passing the remaining 3 to the player on their right. Continue drafting until each player has 4 Leader Cards in hand. Also flip the Personal Bonus Tiles to the advanced side and draft them in reverse turn order instead of using the standard side.
walkthrough.image_caption: Leader cards dealt for drafting

summary.title: Place the Board
bullets: Place the main board centrally
Board shows 4 towers, harvest/production areas, market, council palace, and 3 scoring tracks

summary.title: Sort the Development Cards
bullets: 96 cards in 4 types: Territory (green), Building (yellow), Character (blue), Venture (purple)
Separate by type AND period (I/II/III), shuffle each mini-deck of 8
Stack per type: Period III bottom, Period II middle, Period I top
Place 4 decks near their matching towers

summary.title: Set Up the Excommunication Tiles
bullets: 21 tiles separated by period (I/II/III), shuffle each group
Draw 1 tile per period and place on the matching board space
Return all unused tiles to box unseen

summary.title: Set Up the General Supply
bullets: Wood: 16 small (1 each), 4 large (5 each)
Stone: 16 small (1 each), 4 large (5 each)
Servants: 18 small (1 each), 5 large (5 each)
Coins in 1 and 5 denominations — resources are unlimited

summary.title: Place the Dice
bullets: 3 dice: white, black, orange
Place in designated spaces on the board

summary.title: Adjust for Player Count
bullets: 2 players: place all 4 Covering Tiles
3 players: place 2 small Covering Tiles
4 players: no covering tiles needed

summary.title: Choose Colors and Distribute Player Components
bullets: 1 Personal Board, 1 Personal Bonus Tile (basic side for first game)
3 colored Family Members + 1 uncolored Family Member
3 Excommunication Cubes, 4 Marker Discs

summary.title: Place Marker Discs on Tracks
bullets: 1 disc on VP track at 0, 1 on Military Points at 0, 1 on Faith Points at 0
1 disc on Turn Order track

summary.title: Determine Turn Order
bullets: Randomly choose starting turn order
Place discs on the Turn Order track accordingly

summary.title: Distribute Starting Resources
bullets: All players: 2 wood, 2 stone, 3 servants
Coins by turn order: 1st = 5, 2nd = 6, 3rd = 7, 4th = 8
Later players get more coins to offset going later

summary.title: Advanced Setup: Leader Cards
bullets: Shuffle 20 Leader Cards, deal 4 to each player
Draft: keep 1, pass 3 right; repeat until each player has 4
Flip Personal Bonus Tiles to advanced side, draft in reverse turn order

walkthrough.title: Game Structure: 3 Periods, 6 Rounds
walkthrough.text: Lorenzo il Magnifico is played over 3 periods, each divided into 2 rounds, for a total of 6 rounds. Each round has 4 phases: A) Round Setup, B) Actions, C) Vatican Report (only at the end of rounds 2, 4, and 6), and D) End of the Round. The game gradually escalates as more powerful Period II and Period III cards become available and Vatican Reports demand increasing Faith Points.
walkthrough.image_caption: Overview of the 6-round structure

walkthrough.title: Phase A: Round Setup
walkthrough.text: At the start of each round, draw the top 4 cards from each of the 4 development card decks. Place them on the board from bottom to top in their corresponding towers: green Territory tower, yellow Building tower, blue Character tower, and purple Venture tower. Floor 1 is at the bottom, floor 4 at the top. Then the first player rolls all 3 dice (white, black, orange) and places them on the board in their designated spaces. The values rolled determine the strength of every player's family members for this entire round.
walkthrough.image_caption: Cards placed on tower floors and dice rolled

walkthrough.title: Phase B: Actions Overview
walkthrough.text: Players take turns in turn order. On your turn, you must place one of your 4 family members (3 colored, 1 uncolored) on an available action space and perform the corresponding action. Each of your colored family members has the value of its matching die (white, black, or orange). Your uncolored family member always has a base value of 0. Before placing any family member, you may spend servants to increase its value at a 1:1 ratio without limit. For example, spending 3 servants raises an uncolored pawn from 0 to 3. A family member with a higher value than required can always take an action requiring a lower value. There are 5 areas where you can place family members: the 4 Towers, the Harvest Area, the Production Area, the Market, and the Council Palace.
walkthrough.image_caption: Family members with dice values

walkthrough.title: Action: Taking Cards from Towers
walkthrough.text: The 4 towers each have 4 floors with action spaces. To place a family member on a tower floor, it must meet the minimum value: Floor 1 requires value 1+, Floor 2 requires 3+, Floor 3 requires 5+, and Floor 4 requires 7+ (meaning you always need at least 1 servant or a card bonus for floor 4). There is no required placement order on floors. When you place on a floor, take the card there and pay its cost. Floors 3 and 4 give bonus resources (wood, stone, coins, or Military Points) printed next to the space. You may use these bonuses to help pay the card's cost.
walkthrough.image_caption: Tower floors with value requirements

walkthrough.title: Tower Restrictions
walkthrough.text: Two important restrictions apply to towers. First, you cannot have two family members of the same color in the same tower during a round. Since your uncolored pawn counts as neutral, you can place one colored pawn and one uncolored pawn in the same tower, but never two colored ones. Second, if any family member (yours or another player's) is already in a tower when you want to place there, you must pay 3 additional coins to the supply before placing. You cannot use floor bonus coins to pay this 3-coin penalty. These restrictions create fierce competition for tower spaces.
walkthrough.image_caption: Tower occupation penalty of 3 coins

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Territories (Green)
walkthrough.text: Territory cards have no resource cost to acquire, but you need sufficient Military Points to place them. Your Personal Board's lower line holds up to 6 territories. The first 2 spaces are free, but spaces 3 through 6 require increasing Military Points: you must have (not spend) the required amount shown on your board. Territory permanent effects are activated when you perform a Harvest action, but only if the territory's activation value is equal to or less than your harvest action value. At game end, territories score Victory Points based on how many you have: 3 cards = 1 VP, 4 = 4 VP, 5 = 10 VP, 6 = 20 VP.
walkthrough.image_caption: Territory cards and Military Point requirements

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Buildings (Yellow)
walkthrough.text: Building cards always cost resources (combinations of wood, stone, servants, and coins). Place them on the upper line of your Personal Board from left to right, up to 6. Building permanent effects are activated when you perform a Production action, but only if the building's activation value is equal to or less than your production action value. Building effects typically let you exchange resources for other resources or Victory Points. Important: all resources used for exchange effects must be in your supply before you start the production action. You cannot chain one building's output into another building's input.
walkthrough.image_caption: Building cards with resource costs

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Characters (Blue)
walkthrough.text: Character cards only ever cost coins. Place them in a line beside your Personal Board in the lower right area, up to 6. Character permanent effects are special: they activate in specific circumstances, usually when you perform a particular action. Some give bonus action value when taking certain card types. Some provide cost discounts. Some modify harvest or production values. Their effects can trigger multiple times per round whenever the condition is met. At game end, characters score VP based on total count: 1 = 1 VP, 2 = 3 VP, 3 = 6 VP, 4 = 10 VP, 5 = 15 VP, 6 = 21 VP.
walkthrough.image_caption: Character cards with coin costs

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Ventures (Purple)
walkthrough.text: Venture cards cost either resources, Military Points, or offer a choice between two costs. When a venture requires Military Points, you must have the minimum shown and then spend the reduced amount (moving your disc back). Place ventures in a line beside your Personal Board in the upper right area, up to 6. Venture permanent effects are unique: they only activate at end of game during final scoring. Each venture has its own Victory Point value printed on the card. Your total venture VP is the sum of all your venture cards' VP values.
walkthrough.image_caption: Venture cards with variable costs

walkthrough.title: Card Immediate Effects
walkthrough.text: Most development cards have an immediate effect shown in the middle of the card beside a lightning bolt symbol. When you take a card, resolve its immediate effect right away after paying the cost. Immediate effects can give you resources, points (Victory, Military, or Faith), Council Privileges, or even bonus actions. A bonus action lets you take a card from a tower or perform a Harvest/Production action at a specified value without placing a family member. You still pay costs normally for bonus actions. You may also spend servants to increase a bonus action's value.
walkthrough.image_caption: Immediate effects on development cards

walkthrough.title: Action: Harvest
walkthrough.text: The Harvest area has two spaces. The left space holds only one family member and requires a value of 1 or higher. The right space (unavailable in 2-player games) holds any number of family members but imposes a penalty of -3 to the action value, so you need at least value 4 to use it (since 4 minus 3 equals 1, the minimum). You cannot place two of your colored family members across both harvest spaces, but you can place one colored and one uncolored. When you harvest, your action value determines which territories activate: any territory with an activation value equal to or lower than your harvest value provides its permanent effect. You also receive the harvest bonus from your Personal Bonus Tile, which always activates at value 1.
walkthrough.image_caption: Harvest area with two action spaces

walkthrough.title: Action: Production
walkthrough.text: Production works identically to Harvest in terms of spaces and placement rules. The left space holds one family member at value 1+. The right space holds many but gives -3 penalty. When you produce, your action value determines which buildings activate. Buildings with activation values at or below your production value trigger their permanent effects. Your Personal Bonus Tile's production bonus also activates. Critical rule for buildings: any resources you want to use for exchange effects must already be in your supply before starting production. You cannot use resources gained from one building to feed another building in the same production action. Place resources on the cards before activating to track this.
walkthrough.image_caption: Production area with two action spaces

walkthrough.title: Action: The Market
walkthrough.text: The Market has 4 action spaces (only the first 2 are available in 2-3 player games). Each space holds only 1 family member, but multiple family members of the same color can be in different market spaces. All require a minimum value of 1. The four spaces provide: (1) receive 5 coins, (2) receive 5 servants, (3) gain 3 Military Points and receive 2 coins, (4) receive 2 different Council Privileges (you cannot pick the same privilege twice from one space). The market is your safety net for resources you desperately need.
walkthrough.image_caption: Four market action spaces with their rewards

walkthrough.title: Action: The Council Palace
walkthrough.text: The Council Palace has one action space that can hold any number of family members, even multiple from the same player. It requires a value of 1 or higher. When you place here, arrange family members left to right in order of placement. You immediately receive 1 Council Privilege and 1 coin. The Council Palace also determines next round's turn order: at the end of the round, the first family member placed here makes that player first next round, and so on. Players who did not place in the Council Palace keep their relative order. This makes it a powerful but sometimes overlooked action.
walkthrough.image_caption: Council Palace with turn order implications

walkthrough.title: Council Privileges
walkthrough.text: Whenever you receive a Council Privilege, you choose one of five bonuses: 1 wood and 1 stone, 2 servants, 2 coins, 2 Military Points, or 1 Faith Point. Council Privileges appear frequently — from the Council Palace, from market spaces, from card effects, and from leader cards. When you receive multiple Council Privileges at once (such as 2 from the fourth market space), you must choose different bonuses for each unless a card specifies otherwise.
walkthrough.image_caption: Five Council Privilege options

walkthrough.title: Phase C: Vatican Report
walkthrough.text: This phase only occurs at the end of rounds 2, 4, and 6 — the end of each period. Each period requires a minimum Faith Points level: Period I requires 3 Faith Points, Period II requires 4, and Period III requires 5. If you do not have the required Faith Points, you are automatically excommunicated: place one of your cubes on the current period's excommunication tile and suffer its negative effects for the rest of the game, but keep your faith points. If you do have enough faith, you choose: support the Church (spend all your Faith Points, resetting to 0, and gain Victory Points shown on the Faith track for your current position) or refuse (get excommunicated as above, keeping your Faith Points). At the end of round 6, players who do not support the Church still gain the VP from their faith position after being excommunicated.
walkthrough.image_caption: Vatican Report phase with faith requirements

walkthrough.title: Phase D: End of the Round
walkthrough.text: After all actions (and the Vatican Report if applicable), perform these cleanup steps: Remove all face-up development cards remaining on the board and discard them from the game. Update the Turn Order based on family member placement in the Council Palace. The first family member placed there makes that player go first next round, second goes second, and so on. Players who did not place in the Council Palace maintain their relative order. Finally, return all family members from the board to their owners. You are now ready to begin the next round.
walkthrough.image_caption: End of round cleanup

walkthrough.title: End of Game and Final Scoring
walkthrough.text: The game ends after the 6th round's End of Round phase (including the final Vatican Report). Calculate final scores from these categories: Conquered Territories (1/4/10/20 VP for 3/4/5/6 territory cards), Influenced Characters (1/3/6/10/15/21 VP for 1/2/3/4/5/6 character cards), Encouraged Ventures (sum of VP on all venture card permanent effects), Military Strength (1st place gets 5 VP, 2nd place gets 2 VP; ties share and split), Collected Resources (1 VP per 5 total resources of all types combined, rounding down), plus all VP accumulated on the track during the game. The player with the most Victory Points wins. Tiebreaker: the player higher on the Turn Order track.
walkthrough.image_caption: Final scoring categories

walkthrough.title: Advanced Rules: Leader Cards
walkthrough.text: In the advanced game, Leader Cards add powerful abilities. Each has requirements (not costs) you must have when playing it — such as a certain number of cards, resources, or points. Requirements are not spent; you just need to have them. Leaders have either a Permanent ability (always active once played) or a Once Per Round ability (activate by flipping the card face down; it resets face up at end of round). During the Actions phase, you may perform Leader Actions at any time without placing a family member: play a leader from hand (if requirements met), activate a leader's once-per-round ability, or discard a leader from hand to receive 1 Council Privilege. You may perform multiple leader actions per turn.
walkthrough.image_caption: Leader cards with requirements and abilities

summary.title: Game Structure: 3 Periods, 6 Rounds
bullets: 3 periods of 2 rounds each = 6 rounds total
Each round: A) Round Setup, B) Actions, C) Vatican Report (rounds 2/4/6 only), D) End of Round
Periods escalate with stronger cards and higher faith requirements

summary.title: Phase A: Round Setup
bullets: Draw 4 cards from each deck, place bottom-to-top on matching towers
First player rolls all 3 dice (white, black, orange)
Die values set all players' family member strengths for the round

summary.title: Phase B: Actions Overview
bullets: Take turns in order; place 1 family member per turn on an action space
4 family members total: 3 colored (value = matching die) + 1 uncolored (value = 0)
Spend servants 1:1 to increase any family member's value
5 areas: Towers, Harvest, Production, Market, Council Palace

summary.title: Action: Taking Cards from Towers
bullets: 4 towers, 4 floors each; minimum values: Floor 1 = 1+, Floor 2 = 3+, Floor 3 = 5+, Floor 4 = 7+
Take the card and pay its cost; floors 3-4 give bonus resources
No required order for floor placement

summary.title: Tower Restrictions
bullets: No two same-color family members in the same tower
If any family member is already in a tower, pay 3 extra coins to enter
Cannot use floor bonus coins for the 3-coin tower penalty

summary.title: Card Types: Territories (Green)
bullets: No resource cost, but require Military Points for spaces 3-6 on Personal Board
Permanent effects activate during Harvest action (if value is sufficient)
End-game VP: 3 cards = 1, 4 = 4, 5 = 10, 6 = 20

summary.title: Card Types: Buildings (Yellow)
bullets: Cost resources (wood, stone, servants, coins); placed on upper Personal Board line
Permanent effects activate during Production action (if value is sufficient)
Exchange effects: resources must be in supply BEFORE production starts

summary.title: Card Types: Characters (Blue)
bullets: Only cost coins; placed beside Personal Board lower right
Permanent effects trigger in specific circumstances (action bonuses, discounts)

summary.title: Card Types: Ventures (Purple)
bullets: Cost resources or Military Points (sometimes a choice between two costs)
Permanent effects only score at end of game
Total venture VP = sum of all venture card VP values

summary.title: Card Immediate Effects
bullets: Marked by lightning bolt symbol; resolve when card is acquired
Can give resources, points, Council Privileges, or bonus actions
Bonus actions let you take cards or harvest/produce without placing a family member

summary.title: Action: Harvest
bullets: Left space: 1 family member, value 1+; right space: unlimited, but -3 penalty
Cannot place 2 colored family members across both harvest spaces
Activates territories with value at or below your harvest value, plus Personal Bonus Tile

summary.title: Action: Production
bullets: Same spaces/rules as harvest; activates buildings with sufficient value
Exchange resources must be in supply BEFORE production begins
Cannot chain one building's output into another building's input

summary.title: Action: The Market
bullets: 4 spaces (2 only in 4-player); each holds 1 family member, value 1+
Rewards: 5 coins / 5 servants / 3 Military + 2 coins / 2 Council Privileges
Multiple family members of same color allowed in different market spaces

summary.title: Action: The Council Palace
bullets: 1 space, unlimited family members, value 1+
Receive 1 Council Privilege + 1 coin immediately
Placement order determines next round's turn order

summary.title: Council Privileges
bullets: Choose 1 of 5 bonuses: 1 wood + 1 stone / 2 servants / 2 coins / 2 Military Points / 1 Faith Point
When receiving multiple privileges, must choose different bonuses for each

summary.title: Phase C: Vatican Report
bullets: Occurs end of rounds 2/4/6; requires 3/4/5 Faith Points for Periods I/II/III
Below requirement: excommunicated automatically (place cube, suffer penalty, keep FP)
At requirement: choose to support Church (spend all FP, gain VP) or refuse (excommunicated)
Round 6: excommunicated players still gain VP from faith position

summary.title: Phase D: End of the Round
bullets: Remove leftover development cards from board (discarded from game)
Update Turn Order from Council Palace placement
Return all family members to their owners

summary.title: End of Game and Final Scoring
bullets: Territories: 1/4/10/20 VP for 3/4/5/6 cards
Characters: 1/3/6/10/15/21 VP for 1-6 cards
Ventures: sum of VP on cards; Military: 5 VP 1st, 2 VP 2nd
Resources: 1 VP per 5 total; tiebreak: Turn Order track position

summary.title: Advanced Rules: Leader Cards
bullets: Requirements (not costs) must be met to play; not spent, just possessed
Permanent abilities: always active; Once Per Round: flip to activate, reset at end of round
Leader Actions (free, no family member): play, activate, or discard for Council Privilege

walkthrough.title: Welcome to Florence
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through the first round of a 4-player game. Our four players are the Red, Blue, Green, and Yellow noble families. Each player has 4 family members to place this round. We will demonstrate different actions, show common mistakes, and explain key decisions. Follow along even if you have fewer players — the concepts apply the same way.
walkthrough.image_caption: Four player boards set up and ready

walkthrough.title: Round Setup: Cards and Dice
walkthrough.text: It is the start of Round 1. We draw 4 cards from each deck and place them on the towers, bottom to top. The green tower gets 4 territory cards, yellow gets 4 buildings, blue gets 4 characters, and purple gets 4 ventures. Red is the first player and rolls the dice: white shows 5, black shows 3, orange shows 2. Every player's white family member is now value 5, black is 3, and orange is 2. The uncolored pawns remain at 0.
walkthrough.image_caption: Dice rolled: white 5, black 3, orange 2

walkthrough.title: Red's First Turn: Grabbing a Territory
walkthrough.text: Red goes first. Red notices a good Territory card on the 2nd floor of the green tower (requires value 3+). Red places their black family member (value 3) on that floor. Territories have no resource cost, so Red takes the card for free and places it on the first space of their Personal Board's lower line. The card's immediate effect gives Red 1 wood. A strong opening — Red now has a territory that will generate resources when harvesting.
walkthrough.image_caption: Red places black pawn on territory tower floor 2

walkthrough.title: Blue's First Turn: Acquiring a Character
walkthrough.text: Blue wants a Character card from the 1st floor of the blue tower (requires value 1+). Blue places their orange family member (value 2) there — more than enough. The character costs 3 coins. Blue pays 3 coins from their starting supply of 6 and takes the card. This character has a permanent effect: whenever Blue performs a Harvest action, they get +2 to the action value. That is going to be very useful later.
walkthrough.image_caption: Blue acquires a character card for 3 coins

walkthrough.title: Green's First Turn: The Market
walkthrough.text: Green decides to visit the Market for resources. Green places their orange family member (value 2) on the market space that gives 5 servants. Servants are the most flexible resource in the game because you can spend them to boost any family member's value. Green now has 8 servants total (3 starting + 5 from market). That is a serious war chest for later turns.
walkthrough.image_caption: Green collects 5 servants from the market

walkthrough.title: Yellow's First Turn: The Mistake — Overpaying for a Tower
walkthrough.text: Yellow sees a Building card on the 3rd floor that gives 1 stone as a floor bonus and has a nice permanent effect. Yellow places their white family member (value 5) there. But wait — there is already a family member in this tower (Red's pawn is on floor 2 of the same tower). Oops, that is the territory tower, not the building tower. Actually, let us say Yellow correctly goes to the building tower's 3rd floor. No one else is in the building tower yet, so no 3-coin penalty. Yellow takes the 1 stone bonus and then must pay the building's cost: 3 wood and 1 stone. Yellow only has 2 wood and 2 stone in their starting supply. Yellow cannot afford this card!
walkthrough.image_caption: Yellow realizes they cannot afford the building

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Always Check Card Costs Before Placing
walkthrough.text: Yellow just wasted their strongest family member. This is the number one beginner mistake in Lorenzo il Magnifico: placing a family member on a tower floor before checking whether you can actually pay for the card. Once your family member is placed, you cannot take it back. Always count your resources, check the card cost, and account for any tower occupation penalties before committing. Yellow must now take a less impactful action with their remaining pawns.
walkthrough.image_caption: Check costs before placing

walkthrough.title: Red's Second Turn: The Council Palace
walkthrough.text: Red places their orange family member (value 2) in the Council Palace. Red immediately receives 1 coin and chooses a Council Privilege. Red picks 1 Faith Point — smart thinking, because the first Vatican Report at the end of Round 2 requires 3 Faith Points. Red is also now first in line for turn order next round. Going first is valuable because you get first pick of the new cards.
walkthrough.image_caption: Red enters the Council Palace for privilege and turn order

walkthrough.title: Blue's Second Turn: Taking a Building
walkthrough.text: Blue places their white family member (value 5) on the 2nd floor of the building tower (requires value 3+). The building costs 1 wood and 2 coins. Blue pays from their supply and takes the card, placing it on the upper line of their Personal Board. This building's permanent effect lets Blue exchange 1 wood for 3 coins during production. Blue now has a territory-generating harvest engine from the character card's +2 bonus AND a building ready for production. A solid economic foundation.
walkthrough.image_caption: Blue takes a building card

walkthrough.title: Green's Second Turn: A Costly Tower Entry
walkthrough.text: Green wants a venture card from the purple tower's 1st floor (requires value 1+). Green places their black family member (value 3). But Blue already has a pawn in another tower — wait, actually there are no pawns in the venture tower yet. Green takes the venture card freely. This venture costs 2 Military Points (must have 4 Military Points minimum). Green only has 0 Military Points. Green cannot take this card either! Another player learns the hard way about checking costs.
walkthrough.image_caption: Green cannot meet the Military Point requirement

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Ventures with Military Costs Need Planning
walkthrough.text: Venture cards with Military Point costs are tricky. You need to have the minimum Military Points shown, and then you spend a smaller amount. Many new players forget that ventures require military and try to grab them without enough on the track. Always check the Military Point requirement before placing on a venture. Build up military through market spaces, card effects, and Council Privileges before targeting military-cost ventures.
walkthrough.image_caption: Military Point requirements on venture cards

walkthrough.title: Yellow Recovers: Visiting the Market
walkthrough.text: Yellow needs to recover from their wasted first turn. Yellow places their black family member (value 3) on the market space that gives 5 coins. Now Yellow has resources to work with. Coins are the most versatile resource — they pay for characters, help with tower penalties, and can be part of building costs. Yellow is getting back on track.
walkthrough.image_caption: Yellow recovers with 5 coins from the market

walkthrough.title: Red's Third Turn: Harvest Action
walkthrough.text: Red places their white family member (value 5) on the left harvest space. Red's territory card has an activation value of 3, which is below Red's harvest value of 5, so it activates. Red receives 3 wood from the territory's permanent effect. Red also receives the harvest bonus from their Personal Bonus Tile: 1 wood and 1 stone. In one action, Red gained 4 wood and 1 stone. This is why territories are so valuable — harvest turns them into recurring resource engines.
walkthrough.image_caption: Red harvests for territory effects plus bonus tile

walkthrough.title: Remaining Turns: Uncolored Pawns
walkthrough.text: Each player still has their uncolored family member (value 0). Remember, you can always spend servants to boost this pawn's value. Blue spends 1 servant to raise the uncolored pawn to value 1 and places it in the Council Palace for a Council Privilege plus 1 coin. Green spends 3 servants to raise theirs to 3 and takes a character card from the 2nd floor of the blue tower. Yellow spends 2 servants for value 2 and harvests on the right harvest space (value 2 minus 3 penalty gives a negative, which is not enough). Wait — Yellow needs value 4 minimum for the right space! Yellow must use the left space, but Red is there. Yellow places on the right space and spends 4 servants instead (0 + 4 = 4, minus 3 penalty = 1). That works. Servants are precious — spend them wisely.
walkthrough.image_caption: Uncolored family members being boosted with servants

walkthrough.title: Yellow's Mistake: Ignoring Faith Points
walkthrough.text: After all placements this round, let us check everyone's Faith Points. Red has 1 from the Council Privilege. Blue gained 1 from a card effect. Green has 0. Yellow has 0. Remember: at the end of Round 2, the Vatican Report requires 3 Faith Points. That means everyone needs to gain more faith next round or face excommunication. Yellow has not thought about faith at all and is dangerously behind.
walkthrough.image_caption: Faith Point track showing low totals

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Plan for the Vatican Report Early
walkthrough.text: The Vatican Report is easy to forget but devastating to ignore. Period I excommunication tiles can cripple your strategy for the remaining 4 rounds. You need to start accumulating Faith Points from Round 1 — through Council Privileges (1 Faith Point option), card immediate effects, and character permanent effects. Do not wait until Round 2 to suddenly scramble for 3 Faith Points. Plan your faith income across both rounds of each period.
walkthrough.image_caption: Planning faith accumulation across periods

walkthrough.title: End of Round 1
walkthrough.text: All family members have been placed. Since this is Round 1 (not 2, 4, or 6), there is no Vatican Report. We remove all leftover development cards from the board. We check the Council Palace: Red placed first, then Blue. So next round, Red goes 1st and Blue goes 2nd. Green and Yellow did not place in the palace, so they keep their relative order (Green 3rd, Yellow 4th). All family members return to their owners. Time for Round 2.
walkthrough.image_caption: End of round cleanup and turn order update

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have now seen all five action areas in action, watched Yellow learn from two costly mistakes (not checking card costs and ignoring faith), and seen how territories fuel harvest engines and characters provide ongoing bonuses. The core rhythm of Lorenzo il Magnifico is clear: roll dice, place family members, collect cards and resources, and manage your faith. You are ready to continue on your own.
walkthrough.image_caption: First round complete

walkthrough.title: Before You Begin Your Dynasty
walkthrough.text:
Before you dive into a full game, here are three things that can help.

First — check out General Tips for beginner strategy and the most important priorities each round.

Second — reference the Appendix for quick definitions of every card type, resource, and game term.

And most importantly — ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun building your Renaissance dynasty!

summary.title: Welcome to Florence
bullets: Scripted tutorial with 4 players: Red, Blue, Green, Yellow
Covers a complete first round from setup through end-of-round
Follow along even with fewer players — concepts apply universally

summary.title: Round Setup: Cards and Dice
bullets: 4 cards placed on each tower from bottom to top
First player rolls 3 dice: white 5, black 3, orange 2
All players' family members inherit these values for the round

summary.title: Red's First Turn: Grabbing a Territory
bullets: Red places black pawn (value 3) on territory tower floor 2
Territories cost no resources; card placed on Personal Board
Immediate effect: gain 1 wood

summary.title: Blue's First Turn: Acquiring a Character
bullets: Blue places orange pawn (value 2) on character tower floor 1
Character costs 3 coins — paid from starting supply
Permanent effect: +2 to Harvest action value

summary.title: Green's First Turn: The Market
bullets: Green places pawn on market for 5 servants
Servants boost family member values 1:1
Green now has 8 servants — flexible resource reserve

summary.title: Yellow's First Turn: The Mistake
bullets: Yellow places strongest pawn on building tower floor 3
Cannot afford the card cost (3 wood + 1 stone; only has 2 wood + 2 stone)
Wasted their most valuable family member

summary.title: Lesson: Always Check Card Costs Before Placing
bullets: Count resources, check card cost, check tower penalty BEFORE committing
Once placed, a family member cannot be taken back
The number one beginner mistake in Lorenzo

summary.title: Red's Second Turn: The Council Palace
bullets: Red enters Council Palace: gains 1 coin + 1 Council Privilege
Chooses 1 Faith Point — preparing for Vatican Report
Also secures first-player position for next round

summary.title: Blue's Second Turn: Taking a Building
bullets: Blue takes a building (costs 1 wood + 2 coins)
Building permanent effect: exchange 1 wood for 3 coins during production
Blue building an economic engine across harvest and production

summary.title: Green's Second Turn: A Costly Tower Entry
bullets: Green tries to take a venture with Military Point cost
Green has 0 Military Points — cannot meet the minimum requirement
Another player learns to check costs before placing

summary.title: Lesson: Ventures with Military Costs Need Planning
bullets: Military-cost ventures require minimum MP before you can take them
Build military through market, cards, and Council Privileges first
Always verify the Military Point requirement on venture cards

summary.title: Yellow Recovers: Visiting the Market
bullets: Yellow places on market for 5 coins
Coins are the most versatile resource
Recovery move — back on track after wasted first turn

summary.title: Red's Third Turn: Harvest Action
bullets: Red harvests at value 5; territory (activation 3) activates for 3 wood
Personal Bonus Tile adds 1 wood + 1 stone
One action = 4 wood + 1 stone; harvest is a recurring engine

summary.title: Remaining Turns: Uncolored Pawns
bullets: Uncolored pawns start at value 0; boost with servants (1:1 ratio)
Blue boosts to 1 for Council Palace; Green boosts to 3 for a character
Servants are precious — spend them wisely on value-adding actions

summary.title: Yellow's Mistake: Ignoring Faith Points
bullets: After Round 1: Red has 1 FP, Blue has 1 FP, Green 0, Yellow 0
Vatican Report at end of Round 2 requires 3 Faith Points
Yellow has not thought about faith at all

summary.title: Lesson: Plan for the Vatican Report Early
bullets: Period I excommunication lasts 4 more rounds — devastating
Start gaining faith from Round 1 via Council Privileges and card effects
Plan faith income across both rounds of each period, not last-minute

summary.title: End of Round 1
bullets: No Vatican Report in Round 1 (only rounds 2/4/6)
Leftover cards removed; turn order updated from Council Palace
All family members return to owners; ready for Round 2

summary.title: Congratulations
bullets: You have seen all 5 action areas demonstrated
Yellow made 2 mistakes: not checking costs, ignoring faith
Core rhythm: roll dice, place family members, collect cards, manage faith

summary.title: Before You Begin Your Dynasty
bullets: Check out General Tips for beginner strategy priorities
Reference the Appendix for quick definitions of all game terms
Ask your personal GM Guide anytime — tap Q&A for instant answers

walkthrough.title: Always Check Costs Before Placing
walkthrough.text: The single most important habit in Lorenzo il Magnifico is checking whether you can afford a card before committing a family member. Once placed, your pawn is gone for the round. Count your resources, check the card cost, check if the tower is already occupied (3-coin penalty), and verify Military Point requirements for territories and ventures. This five-second check will save you from the most common and most painful beginner mistake.

walkthrough.title: Plan for the Vatican Report from Round 1
walkthrough.text: The Vatican Report catches new players off guard constantly. You need 3 Faith Points by end of Round 2, 4 by Round 4, and 5 by Round 6. Start accumulating faith immediately. The Council Palace's privilege offers 1 Faith Point. Some character and venture cards give faith as immediate effects. If you fall behind on faith, you will get excommunicated, and Period I excommunication penalties last for 4 more rounds. It is almost never worth intentionally taking early excommunication unless you have a very specific strategy.

walkthrough.title: Prioritize Cards Over Resources Early
walkthrough.text: In the early rounds, development cards are more valuable than raw resources. Cards give you engine pieces that compound over multiple rounds. A territory acquired in Round 1 generates resources every time you harvest for the remaining 5 rounds. A character acquired early provides its bonus across dozens of actions. Resources sitting unused in your supply do nothing. Focus Rounds 1 and 2 on building your card engine, and the resources will follow.

walkthrough.title: Use Your Uncolored Pawn Wisely
walkthrough.text: Your uncolored family member starts at value 0 and requires servants to be useful. However, it is extremely valuable for two reasons. First, it lets you place a second family member in a tower where you already have a colored pawn (since uncolored counts as neutral). Second, it gives you a fourth action each round. Many experienced players save the uncolored pawn for the Council Palace (needs only value 1, costing 1 servant) or for the large harvest/production space. Do not waste it on high-floor tower spaces that would cost too many servants.

walkthrough.title: Servants Are More Valuable Than They Look
walkthrough.text: New players often undervalue servants. They seem boring compared to wood, stone, or coins, but servants are the only resource that directly converts into action power. When the dice roll low, servants rescue your turns. They let your uncolored pawn take meaningful actions. They boost you to reach floor 4 (value 7) for the best cards. The market space that gives 5 servants is one of the strongest early-game plays. Hoard servants when you can.

walkthrough.title: Watch the Turn Order
walkthrough.text: Turn order matters enormously in Lorenzo il Magnifico. Going first means you get first pick of the new development cards and can claim tower spaces before opponents force you to pay the 3-coin penalty. The Council Palace is the only way to change turn order, and placing there early in a round secures the best position. Consider visiting the Council Palace at least once per round, especially if a particularly strong card is likely to appear in the next round's setup.

walkthrough.title: Build Complementary Card Combos
walkthrough.text: The best strategies combine card types that reinforce each other. Territories feed harvest actions, which generate raw materials. Buildings convert those materials into Victory Points during production. Characters boost your action values, letting you reach higher tower floors or activate more cards. Ventures cash in at end game. A character that gives +2 to harvest value means your territories activate more reliably. A building that converts wood to VP means your wood-producing territories become Victory Point engines. Look for these synergies when choosing cards.

walkthrough.title: Do Not Spread Too Thin
walkthrough.text: You only have 4 family members per round and 6 rounds in the game — that is 24 total actions. Every action needs to count. It is better to have a focused strategy with 3-4 strong territories and a couple of buildings than to have 2 of every card type with no synergy. Pick a primary engine (harvest-focused, production-focused, or venture-focused) and support it with complementary cards. You will not have time to do everything, so make deliberate choices about what to pursue and what to ignore.

walkthrough.title: Pay Attention to Other Players' Resources
walkthrough.text: Lorenzo il Magnifico is interactive through tower competition, turn order, and military ranking. Watch what cards your opponents are targeting. If someone is accumulating territories, they will need harvest spaces. If they are low on coins, they cannot buy characters. If they have a pawn in a tower you want, plan for the 3-coin penalty. Blocking an opponent from a critical card can be just as valuable as taking a good card for yourself, especially in the later rounds when scoring differences are tight.

walkthrough.title: Save Your Strongest Pawn for the Best Opportunity
walkthrough.text: Each round, one die will usually roll higher than the others. That family member is your most powerful piece for the round. Do not waste it on a floor 1 action that any pawn could take. Save your highest-value family member for floor 3 or floor 4 tower spaces where the best cards and biggest bonuses live. If the dice roll low across the board, that is when your servant stockpile shines — boost a mediocre pawn to reach the action space you need.

summary.title: Always Check Costs Before Placing
bullets: Count resources, check card cost, check tower penalty, check Military Points
Once a family member is placed, it cannot be taken back
The most common and most painful beginner mistake

summary.title: Plan for the Vatican Report from Round 1
bullets: Faith requirements: 3 (Round 2), 4 (Round 4), 5 (Round 6)
Start accumulating faith immediately — Council Privilege offers 1 FP
Early excommunication penalties last 4+ rounds — devastating

summary.title: Prioritize Cards Over Resources Early
bullets: Cards compound over multiple rounds; idle resources do nothing
A Round 1 territory generates resources for 5 more rounds of harvesting
Focus Rounds 1-2 on engine building; resources will follow

summary.title: Use Your Uncolored Pawn Wisely
bullets: Starts at value 0 — needs servants to function
Can enter a tower where you already have a colored pawn (neutral color)
Best uses: Council Palace (1 servant), large harvest/production space

summary.title: Servants Are More Valuable Than They Look
bullets: Only resource that directly increases action power
Rescue turns when dice roll low; make uncolored pawn useful
5-servant market space is one of the strongest early plays

summary.title: Watch the Turn Order
bullets: First player gets first pick of new cards and avoids tower penalties
Council Palace is the only way to change turn order
Consider visiting the palace at least once per round

summary.title: Build Complementary Card Combos
bullets: Territories + Harvest = raw materials; Buildings + Production = VP conversion
Characters boost action values for better tower/harvest/production results
Look for chains: character +2 harvest makes more territories activate

summary.title: Do Not Spread Too Thin
bullets: 24 total actions in the game (4 per round x 6 rounds)
Focused strategy with synergies beats scattered card collection
Pick a primary engine and support it with complementary pieces

summary.title: Pay Attention to Other Players' Resources
bullets: Watch opponents' card targets, resource levels, and tower positions
Plan for the 3-coin tower penalty when opponents are ahead of you
Blocking a critical card can be as valuable as taking one for yourself

summary.title: Save Your Strongest Pawn for the Best Opportunity
bullets: Use highest-value family member on floor 3-4 for best cards and bonuses
Do not waste high-value pawns on actions any pawn could take
Low dice rolls = time to leverage your servant stockpile

walkthrough.title: The Territory-Harvest Engine
walkthrough.text: One of the most consistent strategies in Lorenzo il Magnifico is building a territory-harvest engine. The goal is to acquire 4 to 6 territories in the first 3 rounds, then harvest repeatedly in the later rounds to generate massive resource income. The key is targeting territories with low activation values (2-4) so they all fire even on modest harvest actions. Pair this with a character that gives +2 to harvest value, and suddenly a value-3 harvest action activates territories up to value 5. The territory VP bonus is enormous: 5 territories scores 10 VP, and 6 territories scores 20 VP. That end-game bonus alone can win the game.
walkthrough.image_caption: Territory engine with harvest synergy

walkthrough.title: The Building-Production Conversion Engine
walkthrough.text: Buildings that convert resources into Victory Points during production are the backbone of a production-focused strategy. Look for buildings that exchange cheap resources (1 wood, 1 stone) for 3-5 VP. Stack 3-4 of these buildings and perform production once or twice per round in the later game. The catch: all exchange resources must be in your supply BEFORE production starts. This means you need a separate resource-generation plan — typically territories that produce wood and stone through harvesting. The harvest-production combo is one of the strongest late-game engines.
walkthrough.image_caption: Building conversion chains for VP

walkthrough.title: The Character Value Amplifier
walkthrough.text: Characters with permanent effects that increase action values are disproportionately powerful. A character giving +2 to harvest effectively makes every future harvest action reach 2 more territories. A character giving +2 when taking building cards means your value-3 family member can access floor 3 (normally needing value 5). Stack two such characters and your effective values become absurd. The best time to acquire these characters is Period I, when they compound across the most remaining rounds. Their 3-coin cost is always worth it when acquired early.
walkthrough.image_caption: Character cards amplifying action values

walkthrough.title: The Military-Venture Rush
walkthrough.text: Ventures scored at end of game can generate 15-25 VP from just 3-4 cards. The military-cost ventures are often the highest value because they are hardest to acquire. The strategy: rush Military Points early through the market space (3 Military + 2 coins) and military-granting card effects. Once you hit 10+ Military Points, you can afford the most expensive ventures while also competing for the 5 VP military first-place bonus. The downside is that Military Points on the track are public information, making you a target for blocking.
walkthrough.image_caption: Military track and venture card synergy

walkthrough.title: Strategic Excommunication
walkthrough.text: Sometimes deliberately taking an excommunication is the right play. If the Period I excommunication tile has a mild penalty (like losing 1 VP per territory) and you are not pursuing territories, the cost of spending 3 Faith Points to avoid it may exceed the penalty itself. Those faith points could instead be spent building toward Period II and III, where penalties are more severe. The key is reading the excommunication tiles during setup and planning your faith strategy around which penalties you can afford to absorb versus which ones would cripple your engine.
walkthrough.image_caption: Evaluating excommunication tile penalties

walkthrough.title: Turn Order Manipulation
walkthrough.text: Advanced players treat the Council Palace as a strategic tool, not just a backup action. Going first lets you take the best Period II and III cards before opponents. It avoids the 3-coin tower penalty on contested towers. It lets you claim the single-occupancy harvest and production spaces. The optimal pattern is: use the Council Palace in rounds where next round's card draw is critical, and skip it in rounds where your current engine needs all 4 family members elsewhere. Timing your Council Palace visits around the card period transitions (Rounds 2 to 3, Rounds 4 to 5) is especially impactful.
walkthrough.image_caption: Council Palace timing around period transitions

walkthrough.title: Leader Card Synergies
walkthrough.text: In the advanced game, Leader Cards are game-defining. During the draft, prioritize leaders whose requirements match your intended strategy. A leader requiring 4 territories pairs perfectly with a territory-harvest engine — you will meet the requirement naturally. Leaders with permanent effects that reduce card costs or increase action values provide ongoing compound benefits. Once-per-round leaders that grant resources or bonus actions are best when their timing aligns with your engine's needs. Discarding unplayable leaders for Council Privileges is always an option and often the right one — a privilege now beats a leader you cannot play until Round 5.
walkthrough.image_caption: Leader cards with strategic requirements

walkthrough.title: Resource Conversion Efficiency
walkthrough.text: At its core, Lorenzo il Magnifico is a resource conversion game. The most efficient players convert the fewest raw inputs into the most Victory Points. Track your VP-per-action ratio. A harvest that generates 5 wood is only valuable if you can convert that wood into VP through building effects or card costs. Servants converting to action value are efficient when they unlock high-floor cards. Coins spent on the tower penalty are inefficient — plan to arrive first. The best players average 3-4 VP per action in the late game through stacked conversion chains.
walkthrough.image_caption: Resource conversion chains and efficiency

walkthrough.title: End-Game Scoring Optimization
walkthrough.text: In the final 2 rounds, shift from engine-building to point maximization. Count your territories and characters to see if adding one more hits a scoring threshold (3 to 4 territories jumps from 1 to 4 VP). Check if you can reach military first place for 5 VP. Convert remaining resources through building effects. Collect ventures with the highest VP values. And critically, calculate whether supporting the Church at the final Vatican Report is worth it — those faith-track VP can swing 3-8 points. The player who best transitions from building to scoring in Rounds 5-6 usually wins.
walkthrough.image_caption: Final scoring calculations

walkthrough.title: Reading the Table and Adapting
walkthrough.text: The strongest Lorenzo il Magnifico players adapt their strategy to what is available and what opponents are doing. If two players are fighting over territories, pivot to characters and ventures where there is less competition. If the dice consistently roll high, servants are less critical; if they roll low, servant hoarding becomes essential. Read the excommunication tiles during setup and build your faith plan before the game starts. Watch which tower spaces opponents are likely to target and decide whether to race them or dodge into open towers. Flexibility beats rigidity in a game with this much variability.
walkthrough.image_caption: Adapting strategy based on game state

Coins on tower penalty are inefficient — plan to arrive first

Military first place = 5 VP; final Vatican Report faith VP = 3-8 points

summary.title: Reading the Table and Adapting

High dice = servants less critical; low dice = servant hoarding essential

entries.definition: The primary scoring currency. Accumulated during the game on the VP track and through end-game bonuses. The player with the most VP wins. Tiebreaker is Turn Order track position.

Tracked on the Military Points track. Required (not spent) to place territories beyond the 2nd space. Spent when acquiring military-cost ventures. At game end, 1st place in military earns 5 VP, 2nd place earns 2 VP.

Tracked on the Faith Points track. Required to avoid excommunication at Vatican Reports (3/4/5 for Periods I/II/III). Supporting the Church spends all faith and grants VP based on current track position.

A basic resource represented by brown wooden tokens. Small tokens = 1, large tokens = 5. Used to pay for buildings and some ventures. Generated by territories during harvest and the Personal Bonus Tile.
A basic resource represented by gray wooden tokens. Small tokens = 1, large tokens = 5. Used to pay for buildings and some ventures. Generated by territories during harvest and floor bonuses.

A flexible resource represented by wooden tokens. Small = 1, large = 5. Spent 1:1 to increase any family member's value. Also used as a resource cost for some buildings. The only resource that directly converts to action power.

A versatile resource in denominations of 1 and 5. Required to buy character cards, pay the 3-coin tower occupation penalty, and as a cost for some buildings and ventures. Starting amount varies by turn order (5-8).

A bonus letting you choose one of five rewards: 1 wood + 1 stone, 2 servants, 2 coins, 2 Military Points, or 1 Faith Point. When receiving multiple privileges at once, you must choose different bonuses for each.

A cylindrical pawn used to take actions. Each player has 3 colored pawns (values matching the white, black, and orange dice) and 1 uncolored pawn (base value 0). Placed on action spaces during the Actions phase.
The 4th family member with a base value of 0 and a neutral color. Can enter a tower that already has one of your colored pawns. Requires servants to be useful. Extremely valuable for its flexibility.

Your player board with spaces for territory cards (lower line, up to 6) and building cards (upper line, up to 6). Shows Military Point requirements for territory spaces 3-6. Also holds your resource supply.
A tile placed next to your Personal Board providing bonus resources during harvest and production actions. Basic side is identical for all players. Advanced side varies and is drafted during setup.

Each player has 3 cubes of their color. When excommunicated during a Vatican Report, place one cube on the current period's excommunication tile. You then suffer the tile's negative effect for the rest of the game.
Each player has 4 discs of their color, placed on the Victory Points track, Military Points track, Faith Points track, and Turn Order track. Moved during the game as points are gained or lost.
Tiles used to block action spaces in 2-player (all 4 tiles) and 3-player (2 small tiles) games. They restrict available tower floors, market spaces, and the large harvest/production spaces.
The 96 cards in 4 types (Territory, Building, Character, Venture) across 3 periods. Acquired from tower action spaces. Each has a cost, optional immediate effect, and permanent effect.
No resource cost but requires Military Points for board spaces 3-6. Permanent effects activate during Harvest. End-game VP: 3 cards = 1, 4 = 4, 5 = 10, 6 = 20. Placed on lower line of Personal Board.
Costs resources (wood, stone, servants, coins). Permanent effects activate during Production — usually exchange resources for VP or other resources. Placed on upper line of Personal Board, up to 6.

Costs only coins. Permanent effects are conditional — trigger when performing specific actions (harvest, production, taking certain cards). Provide action value bonuses or cost discounts. End-game VP: 1-6 cards = 1/3/6/10/15/21.

Costs resources or Military Points (sometimes a choice). Permanent effects score VP only at end of game. Total venture VP = sum of all venture card values. Placed beside Personal Board upper right, up to 6.

Advanced-game cards with requirements (not costs) and powerful abilities. 20 total, drafted 4 per player. Can be played (if requirements met), activated (once per round), or discarded for a Council Privilege. Leader actions do not require placing a family member.

21 tiles across 3 periods. One per period is randomly placed during setup. Players who are excommunicated during a Vatican Report place a cube on the tile and suffer its negative effect for the rest of the game. Effects range from losing VP to restricting actions.

An effect on a development card (marked by a lightning bolt symbol) that resolves once when the card is acquired. Can grant resources, points, Council Privileges, or bonus actions (taking cards or performing harvest/production without placing a family member).

An ongoing effect on a development card shown in the lower portion. Territories activate during harvest; buildings during production; characters in specific circumstances; ventures at end of game only.

An action granted by a card's immediate effect that lets you take a card from a tower or perform a harvest/production at a specified value without placing a family member. All normal rules apply (costs, tower penalties). Servants and character bonuses can modify the bonus action's value.

The minimum action value needed to trigger a territory or building's permanent effect during harvest or production. If your action value is lower than the card's activation value, that card does not activate.

Four vertical structures on the board, one per card type (green/yellow/blue/purple). Each has 4 floors with action spaces requiring minimum values of 1/3/5/7. Floors 3-4 give bonus resources. Two same-color family members cannot occupy the same tower.

When placing a family member in a tower that already contains any family member (yours or an opponent's), you must pay 3 coins to the supply before placing. Floor bonus coins cannot be used for this penalty.

Two action spaces. Left: holds 1 family member, value 1+. Right: holds unlimited family members, -3 penalty to action value (unavailable in 2-player). Cannot place 2 colored pawns across both spaces. Activates territories and Personal Bonus Tile.

Two action spaces with identical rules to the Harvest Area. Activates buildings and Personal Bonus Tile. Exchange resources must be in supply before activation. Cannot chain one building's output into another's input.

Four action spaces (2 only in 4-player games). Each holds 1 family member, value 1+. Same-color pawns may occupy different market spaces. Rewards: 5 coins, 5 servants, 3 Military + 2 coins, 2 different Council Privileges.

One action space holding unlimited family members (even same-color), value 1+. Grants 1 Council Privilege + 1 coin. Placement order determines next round's turn order. A key strategic action for securing first-player position.

Phase occurring at the end of rounds 2, 4, and 6. Players must have 3/4/5 Faith Points for Periods I/II/III or face excommunication. Players meeting the requirement choose to support the Church (spend all FP, gain VP) or refuse (excommunicated, keep FP).

Penalty for failing or refusing to support the Church during a Vatican Report. Place an excommunication cube on the period's tile and suffer its negative effect for the rest of the game. Faith Points are preserved (not spent) when excommunicated.

The game has 3 periods of 2 rounds each. Development cards are divided into periods (I, II, III) with increasing power. Excommunication tiles correspond to periods. Vatican Reports occur at the end of each period.
Phase A of each round. Draw 4 cards per tower deck and place bottom-to-top. First player rolls all 3 dice, setting family member values for the entire round.
A track on the board showing player order. Updated at end of each round based on Council Palace placement. Also serves as the tiebreaker for final scoring. Going first is a significant advantage.
VP awarded based on number of territory cards: 0-2 cards = 0 VP, 3 = 1, 4 = 4, 5 = 10, 6 = 20. The jump from 5 to 6 territories (10 to 20 VP) is the largest single scoring threshold in the game.
VP awarded based on number of character cards: 0 = 0, 1 = 1, 2 = 3, 3 = 6, 4 = 10, 5 = 15, 6 = 21. Triangular number progression — each additional character is worth more than the last.

1 VP for every 5 total resources of all types combined (wood + stone + servants + coins). Rounded down. Typically worth 2-5 VP. Leftover resources are not wasted if you have enough to cross a threshold of 5.

At end of game, the player with the most Military Points on the track earns 5 VP, second place earns 2 VP. Ties: tied first-place players each get 5 VP (no second-place awarded); tied second-place players each get 2 VP.
